_comments: STARTER layout for the OWCS YouTube broadcast overlay — ALL RECTANGLES
BELOW ARE PLACEHOLDER GUESSES. They will not line up until you calibrate.
A rectangle is [x, y, w, h] in the frame's own pixels (origin top-left).
frame_width/frame_height say what resolution those pixels assume; extract
your calibration frames at the same size (OWCS VODs are usually 1920x1080,
and 720p sections scale down by 1.5x — keep one resolution consistent).
Workflow (see docs/layout-calibration.md):
1. extract_calibration_frames.py  -> 10–20 gameplay frames
2. build_layout_debug.py          -> draws these boxes on the frames
3. nudge the numbers below until every box hugs its element
4. build_hero_templates.py        -> crop the hero slots you just aligned
5. rename clean crops to templates/<hero_id>.png
'anchor' recognizes live gameplay (a HUD element that is on-screen during
play but not on break/replay screens). 'replay' is an on-screen marker that
means the current frame is a replay (so it is NOT counted). 'score_map' is
an optional region for future OCR of score/map/timer — it is only drawn by
the debug tool for now and not used for detection.
min_score / match_threshold are 0..1 template-match confidences; raise them
if you get false positives, lower them if good frames are being rejected.
Optional: rename '_reject_example' -> 'reject' to turn on HIGHLIGHT/HIGHLIGHTS frame rejection (needs a -highlight.png crop).

anchor._adjust: Point 'rect' at a HUD element present ONLY during live play (e.g. the round/objective/timer bar). Then create the crop as layouts/owcs_youtube_2026-anchor.png (detect.py can help) and tune min_score.

replay._adjust: Point 'rect' at the on-screen REPLAY marker/badge. If this broadcast has no persistent replay marker, delete this whole 'replay' block; frames just won't be replay-filtered.

score_map._adjust: Optional. Center top score/map/timer plate. Only drawn by build_layout_debug for now (no detection uses it yet).

_slots_note: Five hero portraits per team along the top HUD. slots_a = left/team A, slots_b = right/team B, ordered the way they appear on screen. Each entry is [x,y,w,h]; keep the boxes tight to the portrait art.

_adjust: cut the on-screen HIGHLIGHT banner to
It is a LIST — add more markers (POTG, INTERMISSION, ...).
Use a BROAD rect so matchTemplate finds the banner anywhere in it.
'kind' defaults to 'template'; a future 'text'/OCR kind will read
the words HIGHLIGHT/HIGHLIGHTS anywhere inside the rect.
_reject_example.label: highlight
